\\\"/loqui\\\",\\n    {\\n      schema: {\\n        body: {\\n          type: \\\"object\\\",\\n          required: [\\\"question\\\", \\\"repoPath\\\"],\\n          properties: {\\n            question: { type: \\\"string\\\", minLength: 1 },\\n            repoPath: { type: \\\"string\\\", minLength: 1 },\\n          },\\n        },\\n      },\\n    },\\n    async (request: FastifyRequest<{ Body: LoquiBody }>, reply: FastifyReply) => {\\n      const { question, repoPath } = request.body;\\n\\n      if (!existsSync(repoPath)) {\\n        reply.code(400).send({\\n          error: \\\"Bad request\\\",\\n          message: `repoPath does not exist on this host: ${repoPath}`,\\n        });\\n        return;\\n      }\\n\\n      const answer = await askLoqui(question, repoPath);\\n      reply.send({ answer });\\n    }\\n  );\\n\\n  /**\\n   * POST /tasks/loqui/unified — Unified Loqui intent routing.\\n   *\\n   * Accepts one freeform input string, runs it through the\\n   * classifier + router, and dispatches to the correct backend path.\\n   * The UI sends every Loqui chat message here — there is no more\\n   * \\\"mode\\\" to pick at submit time.\\n   *\\n   * Response shape (always the same top-level wrapper):\\n   *   {\\n   *     route: \\\"build\\\" | \\\"answer\\\" | \\\"clarify\\\",\\n   *     intent, label, reason, confidence, signals,\\n   *     ...plus a route-specific payload:\\n   *       build   → { task_id, run_id, prompt, repo_path }\\n   *       answer  → { answer }\\n   *       clarify → { clarification }\\n   *   }\\n   *\\n   * The `signals` field exists so the UI (and later audit tooling)\\n   * can show *why* Loqui picked the route it picked — this preserves\\n   * the inspectability constraint from the execution-truth work.\\n   */\\n  fastify.post<{ Body: LoquiUnifiedBody }>(\\n    \\\"/loqui/unified\\\",\\n    {\\n      schema: {\\n        body: {\\n          type: \\\"object\\\",\\n          required: [\\\"input\\\", \\\"repoPath\\\"],\\n          properties: {\\n            input: { type: \\\"string\\\", minLength: 1 },\\n            repoPath: { type: \\\"string\\\", minLength: 1 },\\n            context: {\\n              type: \\\"object\\\",\\n              additionalProperties: true,\\n              properties: {\\n                activeRunId: { type: [\\\"string\\\", \\\"null\\\"] },\\n                lastRunId: { type: [\\\"string\\\", \\\"null\\\"] },\\n                lastRunVerdict: { type: [\\\"string\\\", \\\"null\\\"] },\\n                previousMessageWasBuild: { type: \\\"boolean\\\" },\\n              },\\n            },\\n          },\\n        },\\n      },\\n    },\\n    async (request: FastifyRequest<{ Body: LoquiUnifiedBody }>, reply: FastifyReply) => {\\n      const { input, repoPath } = request.body;\\n      const routerContext: LoquiIntentContext = request.body.context ?? {};\\n\\n      if (!existsSync(repoPath)) {\\n        reply.code(400).send({\\n          error: \\\"Bad request\\\",\\n          message: `repoPath does not exist on this host: ${repoPath}`,\\n        });\\n        return;\\n      }\\n\\n      const decision: LoquiRouteDecision = routeLoquiInput({ input, context: routerContext });\\n      console.log(\\n        `[tasks] /loqui/unified: action=${decision.action} intent=${decision.intent} ` +\\n        `label=${decision.label} confidence=${decision.confidence.toFixed(2)} ` +\\n        `reason=\\\"${decision.reason}\\\" signals=${decision.signals.length}`,\\n      );\\n\\n      // Base envelope every response shares. Gives the UI a stable\\n      // shape to hang its intent badge / signal audit off of, no\\n      // matter which backend path actually ran.\\n      const envelope = {\\n        route: decision.action,\\n        intent: decision.intent,\\n        label: decision.label,\\n        reason: decision.reason,\\n        confidence: decision.confidence,\\n        signals: [...decision.signals],\\n        original_input: decision.originalInput,\\n      };\\n\\n      switch (decision.action) {\\n        case \\\"build\\\": {\\n          const result = await submitBuildTask(ctx(), decision.effectivePrompt, repoPath, undefined);\\n          if (result.kind === \\\"needs_clarification\\\") {\\n            reply.send({\\n              ...envelope,\\n              route: \\\"clarify\\\",\\n              status: \\\"needs_clarification\\\",\\n              clarification: result.question,\\n            });\\n            return;\\n          }\\n          if (result.kind === \\\"needs_decomposition\\\") {\\n            reply.code(202).send({\\n              ...envelope,\\n              status: \\\"needs_decomposition\\\",\\n              task_id: result.taskId,\\n              plan: result.plan,\\n              message: result.message,\\n            });\\n            return;\\n          }\\n          reply.code(202).send({\\n            ...envelope,\\n            task_id: result.taskId,\\n            run_id: result.runId,\\n            prompt: result.prompt,\\n            repo_path: result.repoPath,\\n            status: \\\"running\\\",\\n            message: \\\"Build task submitted. Watch the worker grid and Lumen log for progress.\\\",\\n          });\\n          return;\\n        }\\n\\n        case \\\"resume\\\": {\\n          // Stitch the new input onto the most recent tracked run's\\n          // prompt so the Coordinator sees a self-contained request.\\n          // If no prior run exists on the server (e.g. because this\\n          // server was restarted since the UI learned about it), we\\n          // fall back to treating the input as a bare build rather\\n          // than failing — the user's intent to \\\"continue\\\" is clear\\n          // even if we don't have the history to reference.\\n          const requestedPriorId = routerContext.activeRunId ?? routerContext.lastRunId ?? null;\\n          const prior =\\n            await findKnownRunById(ctx(), requestedPriorId) ??\\n            await findLatestKnownRun(ctx());\\n          const stitched = prior\\n            ? `Continue the prior build (${prior.runId}): \\\"${prior.prompt}\\\". Follow-up instruction: ${decision.originalInput}`\\n            : decision.originalInput;\\n          const result = await submitBuildTask(ctx(), stitched, repoPath, undefined);\\n          if (result.kind === \\\"needs_clarification\\\") {\\n            reply.send({\\n              ...envelope,\\n              route: \\\"clarify\\\",\\n              status: \\\"needs_clarification\\\",\\n              clarification: result.question,\\n            });\\n            return;\\n          }\\n          if (result.kind === \\\"needs_decomposition\\\") {\\n            reply.code(202).send({\\n              ...envelope,\\n              status: \\\"needs_decomposition\\\",\\n              task_id: result.taskId,\\n              plan: result.plan,\\n              message: result.message,\\n            });\\n            return;\\n          }\\n          reply.code(202).send({\\n            ...envelope,\\n            task_id: result.taskId,\\n            run_id: result.runId,\\n            prompt: result.prompt,\\n            repo_path: result.repoPath,\\n            resumed_from: prior?.runId ?? null,\\n            status: \\\"running\\\",\\n            message: prior\\n              ? `Resuming from ${prior.runId}. New build task submitted.`\\n              : \\\"No prior run found in persisted history — submitted as a fresh build.\\\",\\n          });\\n          return;\\n        }\\n\\n        case \\\"answer\\\": {\\n          const answer = await askLoqui(decision.effectivePrompt, repoPath);\\n          reply.send({ ...envelope, answer });\\n          return;\\n        }\\n\\n        case \\\"dry_run\\\": {\\n          // Preflight + Dry Run System v1 — grounded structured\\n          // plan produced by generateDryRun. Nothing is written,\\n          // no worker runs. Used by API clients that\\n   * already know they want a plan (no natural-language gate).\\n   * Response shape mirrors the `dry_run` action from the\\n   * unified route: { plan: DryRunPlan }.\\n   */\\n  fastify.post<{ Body: { input: string; repoPath: string } }>(\\n    \\\"/dry-run\\\",\\n    {\\n      schema: {\\n        body: {\\n          type: \\\"object\\\",\\n          required: [\\\"input\\\", \\\"repoPath\\\"],\\n          properties: {\\n            input: { type: \\\"string\\\", minLength: 1 },\\n            repoPath: { type: \\\"string\\\", minLength: 1 },\\n          },\\n        },\\n      },\\n    },\\n    async (request, reply) => {\\n      const { input, repoPath } = request.body;\\n      if (!existsSync(repoPath)) {\\n        reply.code(400).send({\\n          error: \\\"Bad request\\\",\\n          message: `repoPath does not exist on this host: ${repoPath}`,\\n        });\\n        return;\\n      }\\n      const plan = generateDryRun({ input, projectRoot: repoPath });\\n      reply.send({ plan });\\n    }\\n  );\\n\\n  /**\\n   * POST /tasks — Submit a new build task.\\n   *\\n   * Calls coordinator.submit() and tracks the run.\\n   * Returns immediately with task_id + run_id. The run executes async.\\n   * Client should subscribe on WebSocket with { type: \\\"subscribe\\\", runId }.\\n   *\\n   * The optional `repoPath` field in the body lets the caller target a\\n   * specific local repo as the project root for this build. When omitted,\\n   * the Coordinator falls back to its own boot-time config.projectRoot\\n   * (typically the API server's cwd).\\n   */\\n  fastify.post<{ Body: SubmitBody }>(\\n    \\\"/\\\",\\n    {\\n      schema: {\\n        body: {\\n          type: \\\"object\\\",\\n          properties: {\\n            prompt: { type: \\\"string\\\", minLength: 1 },\\n            input: { type: \\\"string\\\", minLength: 1 },\\n            repoPath: { type: \\\"string\\\" },\\n            exclusions: { type: \\\"array\\\", items: { type: \\\"string\\\" } },\\n            quality_bar: { type: \\\"string\\\", enum: [\\\"minimal\\\", \\\"standard\\\", \\\"hardened\\\"] },\\n          },\\n        },\\n      },\\n    },\\n    async (request: FastifyRequest<{ Body: SubmitBody }>, reply: FastifyReply) => {\\n      const prompt = request.body.prompt || request.body.input;\\n      const repoPath = request.body.repoPath;\\n      console.log(\\\"[tasks] POST /tasks received:\\\", { prompt: prompt?.slice(0, 80), repoPath });\\n\\n      if (!prompt || !prompt.trim()) {\\n        reply.code(400).send({\\n          error: \\\"Bad request\\\",\\n          message: \\\"Either 'prompt' or 'input' field is required\\\",\\n        });\\n        return;\\n      }\\n\\n      // Validate repoPath if provided. The CLI does its own existsSync check\\n      // but we re-validate at the API boundary as defense-in-depth — a\\n      // misconfigured client (or a direct curl) might send a path that\\n      // doesn't exist on this host. Failing here gives a clear error\\n      // instead of letting the Coordinator throw mid-build.\\n      if (repoPath !== undefined && repoPath.length > 0) {\\n        if (!existsSync(repoPath)) {\\n          console.warn(`[tasks] rejecting POST: repoPath does not exist: ${repoPath}`);\\n          reply.code(400).send({\\n            error: \\\"Bad request\\\",\\n            message: `repoPath does not exist on this host: ${repoPath}`,\\n          });\\n          return;\\n        }\\n      }\\n\\n      const result = await submitBuildTask(ctx(), prompt.trim(), repoPath, request.body.exclusions);\\n\\n      if (result.kind === \\\"needs_clarification\\\") {\\n        reply.code(202).send({\\n          status: \\\"needs_clarification\\\",\\n          question: result.question,\\n        });\\n        return;\\n      }\\n\\n      if (result.kind === \\\"needs_decomposition\\\") {\\n        reply.code(202).send({\\n          status: \\\"needs_decomposition\\\",\\n          task_id: result.taskId,\\n          plan: result.plan,\\n          message: result.message,\\n        });\\n        return;\\n      }\\n\\n      reply.code(202).send({\\n        task_id: result.taskId,\\n        run_id: result.runId,\\n        status: \\\"running\\\",\\n        prompt: result.prompt,\\n        repo_path: result.repoPath,\\n        message: \\\"Task submitted. Connect to /ws and subscribe to receive live updates.\\\",\\n      });\\n    }\\n  );\\n\\n  /**\\n   * GET /tasks/:id — Get task status and current run state.\\n   */\\n  fastify.get<{ Params: TaskParams }>(\\n    \\\"/:id\\\",\\n    async (request: FastifyRequest<{ Params: TaskParams }>, reply: FastifyReply) => {\\n      const { id } = request.params;\\n      const tracked = trackedRuns.get(id);\\n\\n      if (tracked) {\\n        const activeRun = ctx().coordinator.getRunStatus(tracked.runId);\\n        const completedTasks = activeRun ? activeRun.run.tasks.filter((task) => task.status === \\\"completed\\\").length : 0;\\n        const failedTasks = activeRun ? activeRun.run.tasks.filter((task) => task.status === \\\"failed\\\").length : 0;\\n\\n        reply.send({\\n          task_id: tracked.taskId,\\n          run_id: tracked.runId,\\n          status: tracked.status,\\n          prompt: tracked.prompt,\\n          submitted_at: tracked.submittedAt,\\n          completed_at: tracked.completedAt,\\n          error: tracked.error,\\n          active_run: Boolean(activeRun),\\n          progress: activeRun ? {\\n            phase: activeRun.run.phase,\\n            completed_tasks: completedTasks,\\n            failed_tasks: failedTasks,\\n            total_tasks: activeRun.run.tasks.length,\\n            total_cost: activeRun.run.totalCost,\\n          } : null,\\n        });\\n        return;\\n      }\\n\\n      const persistedTask = await ctx().receiptStore.getTask(id) ?? await ctx().receiptStore.getTaskByRunId(id);\\n      if (persistedTask) {\\n        const persistedRun = await ctx().receiptStore.getRun(persistedTask.runId);\\n        const activeRun = ctx().coordinator.getRunStatus(persistedTask.runId);\\n        const completedTasks = activeRun ? activeRun.run.tasks.filter((task) => task.status === \\\"completed\\\").length : 0;\\n        const failedTasks = activeRun ? activeRun.run.tasks.filter((task) => task.status === \\\"failed\\\").length : 0;\\n        reply.send({\\n          task_id: persistedTask.taskId,\\n          run_id: persistedTask.runId,\\n          status: persistedRun ? toTaskStatus(persistedRun.status) : persistedTask.status,\\n          prompt: persistedTask.prompt,\\n          submitted_at: persistedTask.submittedAt,\\n          completed_at: persistedRun?.completedAt ?? persistedTask.completedAt,\\n          error: persistedRun?.errors[0] ?? persistedTask.error,\\n          active_run: Boolean(activeRun),\\n          progress: activeRun ? {\\n            phase: activeRun.run.phase,\\n            completed_tasks: completedTasks,\\n            failed_tasks: failedTasks,\\n            total_tasks: activeRun.run.tasks.length,\\n            total_cost: activeRun.run.totalCost,\\n          } : persistedRun ? {\\n            phase: persistedRun.phase,\\n            completed_tasks: persistedRun.workerEvents.filter((event) => event.status === \\\"completed\\\").length,\\n            failed_tasks: persistedRun.workerEvents.filter((event) => event.status === \\\"failed\\\").length,\\n            total_tasks: persistedRun.workerEvents.length,\\n            total_cost: persistedRun.totalCost,\\n          } : null,\\n        });\\n        return;\\n      }\\n\\n      // Fallback: search event history\\n      const recentEvents = ctx().eventBus.recentEvents(100);\\n      const taskEvents = recentEvents.filter(\\n        (e) => (e.payload as any).taskId === id || (e.payload as any).runId === id\\n      );\\n\\n      if (taskEvents.length === 0) {\\n        reply.code(404).send({\\n          error: \\\"Not found\\\",\\n          message: `No task or run found with ID \\\"${id}\\\"`,\\n        });\\n        return;\\n      }\\n\\n      const lastEvent = taskEvents[taskEvents.length - 1];\\n      const isComplete = lastEvent.type === \\\"run_complete\\\" || lastEvent.type === \\\"run_receipt\\\";\\n      const runId = (lastEvent.payload as any).runId ?? id;\\n\\n      reply.send({\\n        task_id: id,\\n        run_id: runId,\\n        status: isComplete ? \\\"complete\\\" : \\\"running\\\",\\n        phase: lastEvent.type,\\n        active_run: !isComplete,\\n        progress: null,\\n        last_event: lastEvent,\\n        event_count: taskEvents.length,\\n        events: taskEvents,\\n      });\\n    }\\n  );\\n\\n  /**\\n   * GET /tasks/:id/receipts — Get the full receipt bundle for a completed task.\\n   */\\n  fastify.get<{ Params: TaskParams }>(\\n    \\\"/:id/receipts\\\",\\n    async (request: FastifyRequest<{ Params: TaskParams }>, reply: FastifyReply) => {\\n      const { id } = request.params;\\n\\n      // Check tracked runs first\\n      const tracked = trackedRuns.get(id);\\n      if (tracked?.receipt) {\\n        reply.send({ task_id: id, run_id: tracked.runId, receipt: tracked.receipt });\\n        return;\\n      }\\n\\n      const task = await ctx().receiptStore.getTask(id) ?? await ctx().receiptStore.getTaskByRunId(id);\\n      const persisted = await ctx().receiptStore.getRun(task?.runId ?? id);\\n      if (persisted) {\\n        reply.send({ task_id: task?.taskId ?? id, run_id: persisted.runId, receipt: persisted.finalReceipt ?? persisted });\\n        return;\\n      }\\n\\n      // Fallback to event history\\n      const recentEvents = ctx().eventBus.recentEvents(200);\\n      const receiptEvent = recentEvents.find(\\n        (e) =>\\n          e.type === \\\"run_receipt\\\" &&\\n          ((e.payload as any).taskId === id || (e.payload as any).runId === id)\\n      );\\n\\n      if (!receiptEvent) {\\n        reply.code(404).send({\\n          error: \\\"Not found\\\",\\n          message: `No receipt found for task \\\"${id}\\\". Task may still be running.`,\\n        });\\n        return;\\n      }\\n\\n      reply.send({\\n        task_id: id,\\n        receipt: receiptEvent.payload,\\n      });\\n    }\\n  );\\n\\n  /**\\n   * POST /tasks/:id/cancel — Cancel a running task.\\n   */\\n  fastify.post<{ Params: TaskParams }>(\\n    \\\"/:id/cancel\\\",\\n    async (request: FastifyRequest<{ Params: TaskParams }>, reply: found\\\",\\n          message: result.message,\\n        });\\n        return;\\n      }\\n      reply.send({\\n        task_id: result.taskId,\\n        run_id: result.runId,\\n        status: \\\"cancelled\\\",\\n        message: result.message,\\n      });\\n    }\\n  );\\n\\n  /**\\n   * POST /tasks/:id/approve — Approve a pending decomposition plan and\\n   * resume execution.\\n   */\\n  fastify.post<{ Params: TaskParams }>(\\n    \\\"/:id/approve\\\",\\n    async (request: FastifyRequest<{ Params: TaskParams }>, reply: FastifyReply) => {\\n      const { id } = request.params;\\n      const result = ctx().coordinator.approvePlan(id);\\n\\n      if (!result) {\\n        reply.code(404).send({\\n          error: \\\"Not found\\\",\\n          message: `No pending plan found with ID \\\"${id}\\\"`,\\n        });\\n        return;\\n      }\\n\\n      const taskId = `task_${randomUUID().slice(0, 8)}`;\\n      const runId = randomUUID();\\n      const submittedAt = new Date().toISOString();\\n\\n      const tracked: TrackedRun = {\\n        taskId,\\n        runId,\\n        status: \\\"running\\\",\\n        prompt: `(approved plan ${id})`,\\n        submittedAt,\\n        completedAt: null,\\n        receipt: null,\\n        error: null,\\n      };\\n      trackedRuns.set(taskId, tracked);\\n\\n      result.receipt.then((receipt) => {\\n        tracked.runId = receipt.runId;\\n        tracked.status = receipt.verdict === \\\"success\\\" ? \\\"complete\\\" : receipt.verdict === \\\"aborted\\\" ? \\\"cancelled\\\" : \\\"failed\\\";\\n        tracked.completedAt = new Date().toISOString();\\n        tracked.receipt = receipt;\\n\\n        ctx().eventBus.emit({\\n          type: \\\"run_complete\\\",\\n          payload: {\\n            taskId,\\n            runId: receipt.runId,\\n            verdict: receipt.verdict,\\n            totalCostUsd: receipt.totalCost.estimatedCostUsd,\\n            durationMs: receipt.durationMs,\\n            executionVerified: receipt.executionVerified,\\n            executionReason: receipt.executionGateReason,\\n          },\\n        });\\n      }).catch((err) => {\\n        tracked.status = \\\"failed\\\";\\n        tracked.completedAt = new Date().toISOString();\\n        tracked.error = err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err);\\n      });\\n\\n      reply.code(202).send({\\n        task_id: taskId,\\n        run_id: runId,\\n        status: \\\"running\\\",\\n        message: `Plan \\\"${id}\\\" approved. Execution started.`,\\n      });\\n    }\\n  );\\n\\n  /**\\n   * DELETE /tasks/:id — Cancel a running task.\\n   */\\n  fastify.delete<{ Params: TaskParams }>(\\n    \\\"/:id\\\",\\n    async (request: FastifyRequest<{ Params: TaskParams }>, reply: FastifyReply) => {\\n      const { id } = request.params;\\n      const result = await cancelTrackedTask(id);\\n      if (!result.ok) {\\n        reply.code(404).send({\\n          error: \\\"Not found\\\",\\n          message: result.message,\\n        });\\n        return;\\n      }\\n      reply.send({\\n        task_id: result.taskId,\\n        run_id: result.runId,\\n        status: \\\"cancelled\\\",\\n        message: result.message,\\n      });\\n    }\\n  );\\n};\\n\",\"lineCount\":1067},{\"path\":\"server/index.ts\",\"content\":\"/**\\n * Aedis Server — Fastify + WebSocket on port 18796.\\n *\\n * Entry point for the Aedis API. */\\n\\nimport \\\"dotenv/config\\\";\\n\\nimport { existsSync, readFileSync } from \\\"fs\\\";\\nimport { fileURLToPath } from \\\"url\\\";\\nimport { dirname, join } from \\\"path\\\";\\nimport scripts?: Record<string, string>;\\n      };\\n      scripts = packageJson.scripts ?? {};\\n    } catch {\\n      scripts = {};\\n    }\\n  }\\n\\n  if (typeof scripts.lint === \\\"string\\\" && }));\\n  }\\n\\n  const tsconfigPath = join(projectRoot, \\\"tsconfig.json\\\");\\n  if (existsSync(tsconfigPath)) {\\n    hooks.push(createTypecheckHook({ project: tsconfigPath }));\\n  }\\n\\n  if ──────────────────────────────────────────\\n\\n  const server = Fastify({\\n    logger: {\\n      level: \\\"info\\\",\\n    },\\n  });\\n\\n  // ─── Plugins await server.register(websocket);\\n\\n  // ─── Static files: ui/ at /ui/* ────────────────────────────────\\n\\n  await server.register(fastifyStatic, {\\n    root: UI_ROOT,\\n    prefix: \\\"/ui/\\\",\\n  });\\n\\n  // ─── GET / → serve ui/index.html directly ─────────────────────\\n\\n  const indexHtmlPath = join(UI_ROOT, \\\"index.html\\\");\\n  let indexHtml: string;\\n  try {\\n    indexHtml = readFileSync(indexHtmlPath, \\\"utf-8\\\");\\n  } catch {\\n    indexHtml = \\\"<html><body><h1>Aedis</h1><p>ui/index.html not found</p></body></html>\\\";\\n  }\\n\\n  server.get(\\\"/\\\", async (_request, reply) => {\\n    reply.type(\\\"text/html\\\").send(indexHtml);\\n  });\\n\\n  // ─── Auth middleware ───────────────────────────────────────────\\n\\n  server.addHook(\\n    \\\"onRequest\\\",\\n    createTailscaleAuth({\\n      enabled: !cfg.disableAuth,\\n      allowedCidrs: cfg.allowedCidrs ?? [],\\n    })\\n  );\\n\\n  // ─── Decorate with context ────────────────────────────────────\\n\\n  server.decorate(\\\"ctx\\\", ctx);\\n\\n  // ─── Routes ────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n\\n  await server.register(taskRoutes, { prefix: \\\"/tasks\\\" });\\n  await server.register(runRoutes, { prefix: \\\"/runs\\\" });\\n  await server.register(workerRoutes, { prefix: \\\"/workers\\\" });\\n  await server.register(healthRoutes);\\n  await server.register(configRoutes, { prefix: \\\"/config\\\" });\\n  // Metrics + External API Layer v1 — read-only external surface.\\n  await server.register(metricsRoutes, { prefix: \\\"/metrics\\\" });\\n  await server.register(loquiRoutes, { prefix: \\\"/loqui\\\" });\\n\\n  // ─── WebSocket endpoint ────────────────────────────────────────\\n\\n  server.register(async function (fastify) {\\n    fastify.get(\\\"/ws\\\", { websocket: true }, (socket /* WebSocket */, req) => {\\n\\n      // Parse optional event filter from query\\n      const url = new URL(req.url ?? \\\"/\\\", `http://${req.headers.host ?? \\\"localhost\\\"}`);\\n      const filterParam = url.searchParams.get(\\\"events\\\");\\n      const filter = filterParam\\n        ? (filterParam.split(\\\",\\\") as any[])\\n        : undefined;\\n\\n      eventBus.addClient(socket as any, filter);\\n\\n      // Send welcome message\\n      try {\\n        socket.send(JSON.stringify({\\n          type: \\\"connected\\\",\\n          timestamp: new Date().toISOString(),\\n          message: \\\"Aedis WebSocket connected\\\",\\n          clients: try {\\n          if (socket.readyState === 1) {\\n            socket.ping();\\n          } else {\\n            clearInterval(keepalive);\\n          }\\n        } catch {\\n });\\n\\n      socket.on(\\\"error\\\", () => {\\n        clearInterval(keepalive);\\n        eventBus.removeClient(socket as any);\\n      });\\n\\n      // Handle client messages\\n      socket.on(\\\"message\\\", (data: Buffer) => {\\n        try {\\n          const msg = JSON.parse(data.toString());\\n          if (msg.type === \\\"ping\\\") {\\n            socket.send(JSON.stringify({ type: \\\"pong\\\", timestamp: new Date().toISOString() }));\\n          }\\n          if (msg.type === \\\"subscribe\\\") {\\n            socket.send(JSON.stringify({\\n              type: \\\"subscribed\\\",\\n              taskId: msg.taskId,\\n              runId: msg.runId,\\n              timestamp: new Date().toISOString(),\\n            }));\\n          }\\n        } catch {\\n          // Ignore malformed messages\\n        }\\n      });\\n    });\\n  });\\n\\n  return server;\\n}\\n\\n// ─── Standalone boot ─────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n\\nexport async function startServer(\\n  config: Partial<ServerConfig> = {},\\n  trustProfile?: TrustProfile,\\n  workerRegistry?: server.listen({ port: cfg.port, host: cfg.host });\\n    server.log.info(`Aedis server listening on ${address}`);\\n    server.log.info(`WebSocket available at ws://${cfg.host}:${cfg.port}/ws`);\\n    server.log.info(`UI available at http://${cfg.host}:${cfg.port}/`);\\n  } catch (err) {\\n    server.log.error(err);\\n    process.exit(1);\\n  }\\n\\n  // Graceful shutdown\\n  const shutdown = async () => {\\n    server.log.info(\\\"Shutting down Aedis server...\\\");\\n    await server.close();\\n    process.exit(0);\\n  };\\n\\n  process.on(\\\"SIGINT\\\", shutdown);\\n  process.on(\\\"SIGTERM\\\", shutdown);\\n}\\n\\n// ─── Self-boot when run directly ─────────────────────────────────────\\n\\nconst isDirectRun =\\n  process.argv[1]?.endsWith(\\\"server/index.js\\\") ServerContext;\\n  }\\n}\\n\",\"lineCount\":390},{\"path\":\"server/middleware/auth.ts\",\"content\":\"/**\\n * Tailscale auth middleware — Aedis access control.\\n *\\n * Only accepts connections from Tailscale IP range (100.x.x.x).\\n * All rejected attempts are logged with source IP and timestamp.\\n */\\n\\nimport type { FastifyRequest, FastifyReply, HookHandlerDoneFunction } from \\\"fastify\\\";\\n\\n// ─── Types ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n\\nexport interface AuthConfig {\\n  /** Enable or disable auth (disable for local dev) */\\n  enabled: boolean;\\n  /** Additional allowed CIDRs beyond Tailscale */\\n  allowedCidrs: string[];\\n  /** Log rejected attempts */\\n  logRejections: boolean;\\n}\\n\\nconst DEFAULT_CONFIG: AuthConfig = {\\n  enabled: true,\\n  allowedCidrs: [],\\n  logRejections: true,\\n};\\n\\n// ─── Tailscale IP Check ──────────────────────────────────────────────\\n\\nconst TAILSCALE_PREFIX = \\\"100.\\\";\\n\\nfunction isTailscaleIp(ip: string): boolean {\\n  // Tailscale CGNAT range: 100.64.0.0/10 (100.64.x.x - 100.127.x.x)\\n  // In practice, Tailscale assigns from the full 100.x.x.x range\\n  if (!ip.startsWith(TAILSCALE_PREFIX)) return false;\\n\\n  const parts = ip.split(\\\".\\\");\\n  if (parts.length !== 4) return false;\\n\\n  return parts.every((p) => {\\n    const n = parseInt(p, 10);\\n    return !isNaN(n) && n >= 0 && n <= 255;\\n  });\\n}\\n\\nfunction isLoopback(ip: string): boolean {\\n  return ip === \\\"127.0.0.1\\\" || ip === \\\"::1\\\" || ip === \\\"::ffff:127.0.0.1\\\";\\n}\\n\\nfunction matchesCidr(ip: string, cidr: string): boolean {\\n  const [network, prefixStr] = cidr.split(\\\"/\\\");\\n  if (!network || !prefixStr) return ip === cidr;\\n\\n  const prefix = parseInt(prefixStr, 10);\\n  if (isNaN(prefix)) return false;\\n\\n  const ipNum = ipToNumber(ip);\\n  const netNum = ipToNumber(network);\\n  if (ipNum === null || netNum === null) return false;\\n\\n  const mask = ~((1 << (32 - prefix)) - 1) >>> 0;\\n  return (ipNum & mask) === (netNum & mask);\\n}\\n\\nfunction ipToNumber(ip: string): number | null {\\n  // Strip IPv6 prefix\\n  const clean = ip.replace(/^::ffff:/, \\\"\\\");\\n  const parts = clean.split(\\\".\\\");\\n  if (parts.length !== 4) return null;\\n\\n  let num = 0;\\n  for (const part of parts) {\\n    const n = parseInt(part, 10);\\n    if (isNaN(n) || n < 0 || n > 255) return null;\\n    num = (num << 8) | n;\\n  }\\n  return num >>> 0;\\n}\\n\\n// ─── Extract Client IP ──────────────────────────────────────────────\\n\\nfunction getClientIp(request: FastifyRequest): string {\\n  // Check X-Forwarded-For first (reverse proxy)\\n  const forwarded = request.headers[\\\"x-forwarded-for\\\"];\\n  if (forwarded) {\\n    const first = Array.isArray(forwarded) ? forwarded[0] : forwarded.split(\\\",\\\")[0];\\n    return first.trim();\\n  }\\n\\n  // Check X-Real-IP\\n  const realIp = request.headers[\\\"x-real-ip\\\"];\\n  if (realIp) {\\n    return Array.isArray(realIp) ? realIp[0] : realIp;\\n  }\\n\\n  // Fall back to socket address\\n  return request.ip;\\n}\\n\\n// ─── Middleware Factory ──────────────────────────────────────────────\\n\\nexport function createTailscaleAuth(config: Partial<AuthConfig> = {}) {\\n  const cfg: AuthConfig = { ...DEFAULT_CONFIG, ...config };\\n\\n  return function tailscaleAuth(\\n    request: FastifyRequest,\\n    reply: FastifyReply,\\n    done: HookHandlerDoneFunction\\n  ): void {\\n    if (!cfg.enabled) {\\n      done();\\n      return;\\n    }\\n\\n    const clientIp = getClientIp(request);\\n\\n    // Allow loopback\\n    if (isLoopback(clientIp)) {\\n      done();\\n      return;\\n    }\\n\\n    // Allow Tailscale IPs\\n    if (isTailscaleIp(clientIp)) {\\n      done();\\n      return;\\n    }\\n\\n    // Check additional allowed CIDRs\\n    for (const cidr of cfg.allowedCidrs) {\\n      if (matchesCidr(clientIp, cidr)) {\\n        done();\\n        return;\\n      }\\n    }\\n\\n    // Rejected\\n    if (cfg.logRejections) {\\n      const log = request.log ?? console;\\n      log.warn({\\n        msg: \\\"Rejected non-Tailscale connection\\\",\\n        ip: clientIp,\\n        method: request.method,\\n        url: request.url,\\n        timestamp: new Date().toISOString(),\\n      });\\n    }\\n\\n    reply\\n      .code(403)\\n      .header(\\\"Content-Type\\\", \\\"application/json\\\")\\n      .send({\\n        error: \\\"Forbidden\\\",\\n        message: \\\"Access restricted to Tailscale network\\\",\\n      });\\n  };\\n}\\n\",\"lineCount\":157},{\"path\":\"server/routes/config.ts\",\"content\":\"/**\\n * Config routes — Model assignment management.\\n *\\n * GET  /config/models — Return current model assignments\\n * POST /config/models — Update model assignments\\n *\\n * Persists to .aedis/model-config.json relative to project root.\\n * Falls back to the legacy .zendorium/model-config.json location on\\n * read so existing installs keep working after the rename. New writes\\n * always go to .aedis/.\\n */\\n\\nimport { readFileSync, writeFileSync, mkdirSync, existsSync } from \\\"fs\\\";\\nimport { join } from \\\"path\\\";\\nimport type { FastifyPluginAsync, FastifyRequest, FastifyReply } from \\\"fastify\\\";\\nimport type { ServerContext } from \\\"../index.js\\\";\\n\\n// ─── Types ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n\\nexport interface ModelAssignment {\\n  model: string;\\n  provider: string;\\n}\\n\\nexport interface ModelConfig {\\n  scout: ModelAssignment;\\n  builder: ModelAssignment;\\n  critic: ModelAssignment;\\n  verifier: ModelAssignment;\\n  integrator: ModelAssignment;\\n  escalation: ModelAssignment;\\n  coordinator: ModelAssignment;\\n}\\n\\n// ─── Defaults ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n//\\n// IMPORTANT: a persisted .aedis/model-config.json file at the project\\n// root will OVERRIDE these defaults via loadModelConfig() below (with\\n// .zendorium/model-config.json as the legacy fallback). Changing the\\n// defaults here only affects projects that have no saved config. To\\n// change an active build's model selection, either delete that file or\\n// edit its `builder` / `critic` block directly.\\n//\\n// Per-role fallbacks (Builder + Critic) are NOT in this config — they\\n// are hardcoded in the worker constructors (workers/builder.ts and\\n// workers/critic.ts). The fallback chain logic lives in\\n// core/model-invoker.ts (invokeModelWithFallback).\\n//\\n// Builder default change history:\\n//   - was: claude-sonnet-4-6 / anthropic (briefly tried as primary, but\\n//          ModelStudio turned out to be slow rather than broken)\\n//   - now: qwen3.6-plus / modelstudio (with anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-6\\n//          as the worker-level fallback)\\n//\\n// Critic default change history:\\n//   - was: qwen3.5:9b / ollama\\n//   - now: claude-sonnet-4-6 / anthropic (with ollama/qwen3.5:9b as the\\n//          worker-level fallback) — Critic gates the pipeline so a stronger\\n//          model here pays for itself by catching issues before Verify.\\n\\nconst DEFAULT_MODEL_CONFIG: ModelConfig = {\\n  scout: { model: \\\"local\\\", provider: \\\"local\\\" },\\n  builder: { model: \\\"qwen3.6-plus\\\", provider: \\\"modelstudio\\\" },\\n  critic: { model: \\\"claude-sonnet-4-6\\\", provider: \\\"anthropic\\\" },\\n  verifier: { model: \\\"local\\\", provider: \\\"local\\\" },\\n  integrator: { model: \\\"glm-5.1\\\", provider: \\\"zai\\\" },\\n  escalation: { model: \\\"glm-5.1\\\", provider: \\\"zai\\\" },\\n  coordinator: { model: \\\"xiaomi/mimo-v2-pro\\\", provider: \\\"openrouter\\\" },\\n};\\n\\nconst VALID_ROLES = [\\n  \\\"scout\\\", \\\"builder\\\", \\\"critic\\\", \\\"verifier\\\",\\n  \\\"integrator\\\", \\\"escalation\\\", \\\"coordinator\\\",\\n] as const;\\n\\n// ─── Persistence ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n\\n/** Canonical Aedis state directory. Always used for writes. */\\nfunction configDir(projectRoot: string): string {\\n  return join(projectRoot, \\\".aedis\\\");\\n}\\n\\n/** Legacy Zendorium state directory, read-only fallback during the rename. */\\nfunction legacyConfigDir(projectRoot: string): string {\\n  return join(projectRoot, \\\".zendorium\\\");\\n}\\n\\nfunction configPath(projectRoot: string): string {\\n  return join(configDir(projectRoot), \\\"model-config.json\\\");\\n}\\n\\nfunction legacyConfigPath(projectRoot: string): string {\\n  return join(legacyConfigDir(projectRoot), \\\"model-config.json\\\");\\n}\\n\\nexport function loadModelConfig(projectRoot: string): ModelConfig {\\n  // Prefer the canonical .aedis/ path. Fall back to the legacy\\n  // .zendorium/ path so installs that were configured before the\\n  // rename still load their saved assignments. Writes always go to\\n  // the canonical path, so the legacy file stops being read the next\\n  // time the config is saved.\\n  const canonical = configPath(projectRoot);\\n  const legacy = legacyConfigPath(projectRoot);\\n  const pathsToTry = [canonical, legacy];\\n\\n  for (const path of pathsToTry) {\\n    try {\\n      if (existsSync(path)) {\\n        const raw = readFileSync(path, \\\"utf-8\\\");\\n        const parsed = JSON.parse(raw);\\n        // Merge with defaults so new roles get default values\\n        return { ...DEFAULT_MODEL_CONFIG, ...parsed };\\n      }\\n    } catch {\\n      // Corrupt file — try the next candidate, or fall through to defaults.\\n    }\\n  }\\n  return { ...DEFAULT_MODEL_CONFIG };\\n}\\n\\nfunction saveModelConfig(projectRoot: string, config: ModelConfig): void {\\n  const dir = configDir(projectRoot);\\n  if (!existsSync(dir)) {\\n    mkdirSync(dir, { recursive: true });\\n  }\\n  writeFileSync(configPath(projectRoot), JSON.stringify(config, null, 2) + \\\"\\\\n\\\", \\\"utf-8\\\");\\n}\\n\\nfunction validateModelConfig(body: unknown): { valid: boolean; errors: string[] } {\\n  const errors: string[] = [];\\n  if (typeof body !== \\\"object\\\" || body === null) {\\n    return { valid: false, errors: [\\\"Body must be a JSON object\\\"] };\\n  }\\n\\n  const obj = body as Record<string, unknown>;\\n\\n  for (const role of VALID_ROLES) {\\n    if (role in obj) {\\n      const entry = obj[role] as any;\\n      if (typeof entry !== \\\"object\\\" || entry === null) {\\n        errors.push(`${role} must be an object with { model, provider }`);\\n        continue;\\n      }\\n      if (typeof entry.model !== \\\"string\\\" || !entry.model.trim()) {\\n        errors.push(`${role}.model must be a non-empty string`);\\n      }\\n      if (typeof entry.provider !== \\\"string\\\" || !entry.provider.trim()) {\\n        errors.push(`${role}.provider must be a non-empty string`);\\n      }\\n    }\\n  }\\n\\n  // Warn about unknown keys but don't fail\\n  for (const key of Object.keys(obj)) {\\n    if (!VALID_ROLES.includes(key as any)) {\\n      errors.push(`Unknown role \\\"${key}\\\" — ignored`);\\n    }\\n  }\\n\\n  return { valid: errors.filter((e) => !e.includes(\\\"ignored\\\")).length === 0, errors };\\n}\\n\\n// ─── Routes ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n\\nexport const configRoutes: FastifyPluginAsync = async (fastify) => {\\n  const ctx = (): ServerContext => fastify.ctx;\\n\\n  /**\\n   * GET /config/models — Return current model assignments.\\n   */\\n  fastify.get(\\n    \\\"/models\\\",\\n    async (_request: FastifyRequest, reply: FastifyReply) => {\\n      const config = loadModelConfig(ctx().config.projectRoot);\\n      reply.send({\\n        models: config,\\n        config_path: configPath(ctx().config.projectRoot),\\n        roles: VALID_ROLES,\\n      });\\n    }\\n  );\\n\\n  /**\\n   * POST /config/models — Update model assignments.\\n   * Accepts a partial or full ModelConfig. Missing roles keep their current values.\\n   */\\n  fastify.post<{ Body: Partial<ModelConfig> }>(\\n    \\\"/models\\\",\\n    async (request: FastifyRequest<{ Body: Partial<ModelConfig> }>, reply: FastifyReply) => {\\n      const { valid, errors } = validateModelConfig(request.body);\\n\\n      if (!valid) {\\n        reply.code(400).send({\\n          error: \\\"Validation failed\\\",\\n          errors,\\n        });\\n        return;\\n      }\\n\\n      const projectRoot = ctx().config.projectRoot;\\n      const current = loadModelConfig(projectRoot);\\n\\n      // Merge incoming assignments with current config\\n      const updated: ModelConfig = { ...current };\\n      for (const role of VALID_ROLES) {\\n        if (role in (request.body as any)) {\\n          (updated as any)[role] = (request.body as any)[role];\\n        }\\n      }\\n\\n      saveModelConfig(projectRoot, updated);\\n\\n      // Emit config change event\\n      ctx().eventBus.emit({\\n        type: \\\"config_event\\\",\\n        payload: {\\n          kind: \\\"config_update\\\",\\n          summary: \\\"Model configuration updated\\\",\\n          models: updated,\\n        },\\n      });\\n\\n      reply.send({\\n        models: updated,\\n        updated_roles: VALID_ROLES.filter((role) => role in (request.body as any)),\\n        message: \\\"Model configuration saved. Changes take effect on next run.\\\",\\n        warnings: errors.filter((e) => e.includes(\\\"ignored\\\")),\\n      });\\n    }\\n  );\\n};\\n\",\"lineCount\":232},{\"path\":\"server/routes/health.ts\",\"content\":\"/**\\n * Health route — System readiness check.\\n *\\n * GET /health — Reports system status:\\n *   - Server uptime\\n *   - Crucibulum connection status\\n *   - Worker availability per role\\n *   - Port status\\n *   - WebSocket client count\\n */\\n\\nimport type { FastifyPluginAsync, FastifyRequest, FastifyReply } from \\\"fastify\\\";\\nimport type { ServerContext } from \\\"../index.js\\\";\\nimport type { WorkerType } from \\\"../../workers/base.js\\\";\\n\\n// ─── Routes ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n\\nexport const healthRoutes: FastifyPluginAsync = async (fastify) => {\\n  const ctx = (): ServerContext => fastify.ctx;\\n\\n  const WORKER_TYPES: WorkerType[] = [\\\"scout\\\", \\\"builder\\\", \\\"critic\\\", \\\"verifier\\\", \\\"integrator\\\"];\\n\\n  /**\\n   * GET /health — Full system health check.\\n   */\\n  fastify.get(\\n    \\\"/health\\\",\\n    async (_request: FastifyRequest, reply: FastifyReply) => {\\n      const context = ctx();\\n\\n      // Worker availability\\n      const workerStatus: Record<string, { available: boolean; count: number }> = {};\\n      let allWorkersAvailable = true;\\n\\n      for (const type of WORKER_TYPES) {\\n        const workers = context.workerRegistry.getWorkers(type);\\n        workerStatus[type] = {\\n          available: workers.length > 0,\\n          count: workers.length,\\n        };\\n        if (workers.length === 0) allWorkersAvailable = false;\\n      }\\n\\n      // Uptime\\n      const startedAt = new Date(context.startedAt);\\n      const uptimeMs = Date.now() - startedAt.getTime();\\n\\n      // WebSocket clients\\n      const wsClients = context.eventBus.clientCount();\\n\\n      // Overall status\\n      const status = allWorkersAvailable ? Returns a grounded answer plus\\n * the list of repo files the gated context layer flagged as\\n * relevant to the question, and a rough confidence score so\\n * callers can tell \\\"I found context and got an answer\\\" apart\\n * from \\\"I had no relevant context.\\\"\\n *\\n * Design principles:\\n *\\n *   1. \\\"\\\").trim();\\n      const repoPath = String(request.query.repoPath ?? \\\"\\\").trim();\\n\\n      if (!question) {\\n        reply.code(400).send({\\n          error: \\\"Bad request\\\",\\n          message: \\\"The `question` query parameter is required.\\\",\\n        });\\n        return;\\n      }\\n      if (!repoPath) {\\n        reply.code(400).send({\\n          error: \\\"Bad request\\\",\\n          message: \\\"The `repoPath` query parameter is required.\\\",\\n        });\\n        return;\\n      }\\n      if (!existsSync(repoPath)) {\\n        reply.code(400).send({\\n          error: \\\"Bad request\\\",\\n          message: `repoPath does not exist on this host: ${repoPath}`,\\n        });\\n        return;\\n      }\\n\\n      const result = await answerLoqui(question, repoPath);\\n\\n      reply.send({\\n        answer: result.answer,\\n        confidence: result.confidence,\\n        relatedFiles: result.relatedFiles,\\n        reason: result.reason,\\n        provider: result.provider,\\n      });\\n    },\\n  );\\n};\\n\",\"lineCount\":89},{\"path\":\"server/routes/metrics.ts\",\"content\":\"/**\\n * Metrics route — Metrics + External API Layer v1.\\n *\\n * GET /metrics — aggregate snapshot across every tracked run.\\n *\\n * Read-only. [] },\\n   });\",\"originalContent\":\"/**\\n * Task routes — Submit, query, and cancel build tasks.\\n *\\n * POST /tasks       — Submit a new task, returns { task_id, run_id }\\n * GET  /tasks/:id   — Status + current run state\\n * GET  /tasks/:id/receipts — Full receipt bundle\\n * POST /tasks/:id/cancel — Cancel a running task\\n * DELETE /tasks/:id — Cancel a running task\\n */\\n\\nimport { randomUUID } from \\\"crypto\\\";\\nimport { existsSync } from \\\"fs\\\";\\nimport type { FastifyPluginAsync, FastifyRequest, FastifyReply } from \\\"fastify\\\";\\nimport { askLoqui } from \\\"../../core/loqui.js\\\";\\nimport { routeLoquiInput, type LoquiRouteDecision } from \\\"../../core/loqui-router.js\\\";\\nimport type { LoquiIntentContext } from \\\"../../core/loqui-intent.js\\\";\\nimport { generateDryRun } from \\\"../../core/dry-run.js\\\";\\nimport type { ServerContext } from \\\"../index.js\\\";\\n\\n// ─── Request/Response Schemas ────────────────────────────────────────\\n\\ninterface SubmitBody {\\n  /** Natural language prompt — also accepted as \\\"input\\\" */\\n  prompt?: string;\\n  input?: string;\\n  /** Repo path to operate on */\\n  repoPath?: string;\\n  exclusions?: string[];\\n  quality_bar?: \\\"minimal\\\" | \\\"standard\\\" | \\\"hardened\\\";\\n}\\n\\ninterface TaskParams {\\n  id: string;\\n}\\n\\ninterface LoquiBody {\\n  question: string;\\n  repoPath: string;\\n}\\n\\n/**\\n * Unified Loqui route request body. A single freeform input stream\\n * that the classifier will route to build / answer / resume /\\n * clarify. The `context` field is optional — the UI passes it when\\n * it knows about a prior run so the classifier can emit resume_run\\n * or status intents safely (see core/loqui-intent.ts Rule B).\\n */\\ninterface LoquiUnifiedBody {\\n  input: string;\\n  repoPath: string;\\n  context?: LoquiIntentContext;\\n}\\n\\n// ─── In-memory run tracker (bridges POST → Coordinator → WS) ────────\\n\\ninterface TrackedRun {\\n  taskId: string;\\n  runId: string;\\n  status: \\\"queued\\\" | \\\"running\\\" | \\\"complete\\\" | \\\"failed\\\" | \\\"cancelled\\\";\\n  prompt: string;\\n  submittedAt: string;\\n  completedAt: string | null;\\n  receipt: unknown | null;\\n  error: string | null;\\n}\\n\\nconst trackedRuns = new Map<string, TrackedRun>();\\n\\nexport function getTrackedRun(taskId: string): TrackedRun | undefined {\\n  return trackedRuns.get(taskId);\\n}\\n\\n/**\\n * Snapshot of every tracked run, newest first. Read-only — returns\\n * a fresh array so callers can iterate / sort / slice without\\n * touching the internal Map. Used by the external API layer\\n * (Metrics + External API v1) so /metrics, /runs, and /runs/:id\\n * can aggregate across the same registry that /tasks writes to,\\n * without importing the private Map or creating a second source\\n * of truth.\\n */\\nexport function getAllTrackedRuns(): readonly TrackedRun[] {\\n  const out = [...trackedRuns.values()];\\n  out.sort((a, b) => (a.submittedAt < b.submittedAt ? 1 : -1));\\n  return out;\\n}\\n\\n/** Exported type for the external API routes. */\\nexport type { TrackedRun };\\n\\n/**\\n * Result from submitBuildTask — may be a running build, a clarification\\n * request, or a decomposition plan.\\n */\\ntype BuildSubmitResult =\\n  | { kind: \\\"running\\\"; taskId: string; runId: string; prompt: string; repoPath: string | null }\\n  | { kind: \\\"needs_clarification\\\"; question: string }\\n  | { kind: \\\"needs_decomposition\\\"; taskId: string; plan: unknown; message: string };\\n\\n/**\\n * Shared build-submit helper. id;\\n\\n      reply.send({\\n        task_id: id,\\n        run_id: runId,\\n        status: isComplete ? \\\"complete\\\" : \\\"running\\\",\\n        phase: lastEvent.type,\\n        active_run: !isComplete,\\n        progress: null,\\n        last_event: lastEvent,\\n        event_count: taskEvents.length,\\n        events: taskEvents,\\n      });\\n    }\\n  );\\n\\n  /**\\n   * GET /tasks/:id/receipts — Get the full receipt bundle for a completed task.\\n   */\\n  fastify.get<{ Params: TaskParams }>(\\n    \\\"/:id/receipts\\\",\\n    async (request: FastifyRequest<{ Params: TaskParams }>, reply: FastifyReply) => {\\n      const { id } = request.params;\\n\\n      // Check tracked runs first\\n      const tracked = trackedRuns.get(id);\\n      if (tracked?.receipt) {\\n        reply.send({ task_id: id, run_id: tracked.runId, receipt: tracked.receipt });\\n        return;\\n      }\\n\\n      const task = await ctx().receiptStore.getTask(id) ?? await ctx().receiptStore.getTaskByRunId(id);\\n      const persisted = await ctx().receiptStore.getRun(task?.runId ?? id);\\n      if (persisted) {\\n        reply.send({ task_id: task?.taskId ?? id, run_id: persisted.runId, receipt: persisted.finalReceipt ?? persisted });\\n        return;\\n      }\\n\\n      // Fallback to event history\\n      const recentEvents = ctx().eventBus.recentEvents(200);\\n      const receiptEvent = recentEvents.find(\\n        (e) =>\\n          e.type === \\\"run_receipt\\\" &&\\n          ((e.payload as any).taskId === id || (e.payload as any).runId === id)\\n      );\\n\\n      if (!receiptEvent) {\\n        reply.code(404).send({\\n          error: \\\"Not found\\\",\\n          message: `No receipt found for task \\\"${id}\\\". Task may still be running.`,\\n        });\\n        return;\\n      }\\n\\n      reply.send({\\n        task_id: id,\\n        receipt: receiptEvent.payload,\\n      });\\n    }\\n  );\\n\\n  /**\\n   * POST /tasks/:id/cancel — Cancel a running task.\\n   */\\n  fastify.post<{ Params: TaskParams }>(\\n    \\\"/:id/cancel\\\",\\n    async (request: FastifyRequest<{ Params: TaskParams }>, reply: FastifyReply) => {\\n      const { id } = request.params;\\n      const result = await cancelTrackedTask(id);\\n      if (!result.ok) {\\n        reply.code(404).send({\\n          error: \\\"Not found\\\",\\n          message: result.message,\\n        });\\n        return;\\n      }\\n      reply.send({\\n        task_id: result.taskId,\\n        run_id: result.runId,\\n        status: \\\"cancelled\\\",\\n        message: result.message,\\n      });\\n    }\\n  );\\n\\n  /**\\n   * POST /tasks/:id/approve — Approve a pending decomposition plan and\\n   * resume execution.\\n   */\\n  fastify.post<{ Params: TaskParams }>(\\n    \\\"/:id/approve\\\",\\n    async (request: FastifyRequest<{ Params: TaskParams }>, reply: FastifyReply) => {\\n      const { id } = request.params;\\n      const result = ctx().coordinator.approvePlan(id);\\n\\n      if (!result) {\\n        reply.code(404).send({\\n          error: \\\"Not found\\\",\\n          message: `No pending plan found with ID \\\"${id}\\\"`,\\n        });\\n        return;\\n      }\\n\\n      const taskId = `task_${randomUUID().slice(0, 8)}`;\\n      const runId = randomUUID();\\n      const submittedAt = new Date().toISOString();\\n\\n      const tracked: TrackedRun = {\\n        taskId,\\n        runId,\\n        status: \\\"running\\\",\\n        prompt: `(approved plan ${id})`,\\n        submittedAt,\\n        completedAt: null,\\n        receipt: null,\\n        error: null,\\n      };\\n      trackedRuns.set(taskId, tracked);\\n\\n      result.receipt.then((receipt) => {\\n        tracked.runId = receipt.runId;\\n        tracked.status = receipt.verdict === \\\"success\\\" ? \\\"complete\\\" : receipt.verdict === \\\"aborted\\\" ? \\\"cancelled\\\" : \\\"failed\\\";\\n        tracked.completedAt = new Date().toISOString();\\n        tracked.receipt = receipt;\\n\\n        ctx().eventBus.emit({\\n          type: \\\"run_complete\\\",\\n          payload: {\\n            taskId,\\n            runId: receipt.runId,\\n            verdict: receipt.verdict,\\n            totalCostUsd: receipt.totalCost.estimatedCostUsd,\\n            durationMs: receipt.durationMs,\\n \\\"failed\\\";\\n        tracked.completedAt = new Date().toISOString();\\n        tracked.error = err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err);\\n      });\\n\\n      reply.code(202).send({\\n        task_id: taskId,\\n        run_id: runId,\\n        status: \\\"running\\\",\\n        message: `Plan \\\"${id}\\\" approved. Execution started.`,\\n      });\\n    }\\n  );\\n\\n  /**\\n   * DELETE /tasks/:id — Cancel a running task.\\n   */\\n  fastify.delete<{ Params: TaskParams }>(\\n    \\\"/:id\\\",\\n    async (request: FastifyRequest<{ Params: TaskParams }>, reply: FastifyReply) => {\\n      const { id } = request.params;\\n      const result = await cancelTrackedTask(id);\\n      if (!result.ok) {\\n build tasks.\\n *\\n * POST /tasks       — Submit a new task, returns { task_id, run_id }\\n * GET  /tasks/:id   — Status + current run state\\n * GET  /tasks/:id/receipts — Full receipt bundle\\n * POST /tasks/:id/cancel — Cancel a running task\\n * DELETE /tasks/:id — Cancel a running task\\n */\\n\\nimport { randomUUID } from \\\"crypto\\\";\\nimport { existsSync } from \\\"fs\\\";\\nimport type { FastifyPluginAsync, FastifyRequest, FastifyReply } from \\\"fastify\\\";\\nimport { askLoqui } from \\\"../../core/loqui.js\\\";\\nimport { routeLoquiInput, type LoquiRouteDecision } from ServerContext } from \\\"../index.js\\\";\\n\\n// ─── Request/Response Schemas ────────────────────────────────────────\\n\\ninterface SubmitBody {\\n  /** Natural language prompt — also accepted as \\\"input\\\" */\\n  prompt?: string;\\n  input?: string;\\n  /** Repo path to operate on */\\n  repoPath?: string;\\n  exclusions?: string[];\\n  quality_bar?: \\\"minimal\\\" | \\\"standard\\\" | \\\"hardened\\\";\\n}\\n\\ninterface TaskParams {\\n  id: string;\\n}\\n\\ninterface LoquiBody {\\n  question: string;\\n  repoPath: string;\\n}\\n\\n/**\\n * Unified Loqui route request body. The `context` field is optional — the UI passes it when\\n * it knows about a prior run so the classifier can emit resume_run\\n * or status intents safely (see core/loqui-intent.ts Rule B).\\n */\\ninterface LoquiUnifiedBody {\\n  input: string;\\n  repoPath: string;\\n  context?: LoquiIntentContext;\\n}\\n\\n// ─── In-memory run tracker (bridges POST → Coordinator → WS) ────────\\n\\ninterface TrackedRun {\\n  taskId: string;\\n  runId: string;\\n  status: \\\"queued\\\" | \\\"running\\\" | \\\"complete\\\" | \\\"failed\\\" | \\\"cancelled\\\";\\n  prompt: string;\\n  submittedAt: string;\\n  completedAt: string | null;\\n  receipt: unknown | null;\\n  error: string | null;\\n}\\n\\nconst trackedRuns = new Map<string, TrackedRun>();\\n\\nexport function getTrackedRun(taskId: string): TrackedRun | undefined {\\n  return trackedRuns.get(taskId);\\n}\\n\\n/**\\n * Snapshot of every tracked run, newest first. Read-only — returns\\n * a fresh array so callers can iterate / sort / slice without\\n * touching the internal Map. Used by the external API layer\\n * (Metrics + External API v1) so /metrics, /runs, and /runs/:id\\n * can aggregate across the same registry that /tasks writes to,\\n * without importing the private Map or creating a second source\\n * of truth.\\n */\\nexport function getAllTrackedRuns(): readonly TrackedRun[] {\\n  const out = [...trackedRuns.values()];\\n  out.sort((a, b) => (a.submittedAt < b.submittedAt ? 1 : -1));\\n  return out;\\n}\\n\\n/** Exported type for the external API routes. */\\nexport type { TrackedRun };\\n\\n/**\\n * Result from submitBuildTask — may be a running build, a clarification\\n * request, or a decomposition plan.\\n */\\ntype BuildSubmitResult =\\n  | { kind: \\\"running\\\"; taskId: string; runId: string; prompt: string; repoPath: string | null }\\n  | { kind: \\\"needs_clarification\\\"; question: string }\\n  | { kind: \\\"needs_decomposition\\\"; taskId: string; plan: unknown; message: string };\\n\\n/**\\n * Shared build-submit helper. Dispatches a build task through the\\n * Coordinator (via submitWithGates) and registers the tracked run state.\\n * Used by both the legacy POST /tasks handler and the new unified POST\\n * /tasks/loqui path so we get exactly one code path for \\\"start a build.\\\"\\n *\\n * Now returns a discriminated union so callers can handle clarification\\n * and decomposition gates without starting execution.\\n */\\nasync function submitBuildTask(\\n  ctx: ServerContext,\\n  prompt: string,\\n  repoPath: string | undefined,\\n  exclusions: string[] | undefined,\\n): Promise<BuildSubmitResult> {\\n  const taskId = `task_${randomUUID().slice(0, 8)}`;\\n  const runId = randomUUID();\\n  const submittedAt = new Date().toISOString();\\n\\n  // Run pre-execution gates (ambiguity + decomposition)\\n  const gateResult = await ctx.coordinator.submitWithGates({\\n    runId,\\n    input: prompt,\\n    exclusions,\\n    ...(repoPath ? { projectRoot: repoPath } : {}),\\n  });\\n\\n  if (gateResult.kind === \\\"needs_clarification\\\") {\\n    console.log(`[tasks] clarification needed for prompt: \\\"${prompt.slice(0, 80)}\\\"`);\\n    return { kind: \\\"needs_clarification\\\", question: gateResult.question };\\n  }\\n\\n  if (gateResult.kind === \\\"needs_decomposition\\\") {\\n    console.log(`[tasks] decomposition needed — ${(gateResult.plan as any).waves?.length ?? 0} wave(s)`);\\n    return {\\n      kind: \\\"needs_decomposition\\\",\\n      taskId,\\n      plan: gateResult.plan,\\n      message: gateResult.message,\\n    };\\n  }\\n\\n  // Gate passed — execution started\\n  const tracked: TrackedRun = {\\n    taskId,\\n    runId,\\n    status: \\\"queued\\\",\\n    prompt,\\n    submittedAt,\\n    completedAt: null,\\n    receipt: null,\\n    error: null,\\n  };\\n  trackedRuns.set(taskId, tracked);\\n  await ctx.receiptStore.registerTask({\\n    taskId,\\n    runId,\\n    prompt: tracked.prompt,\\n    submittedAt,\\n  });\\n  tracked.status = \\\"running\\\";\\n  void ctx.receiptStore.updateTask(taskId, { status: \\\"running\\\" }).catch((err: unknown) =>\\n    console.error(\\\"[tasks] receiptStore update failed:\\\", err),\\n  );\\n\\n  ctx.eventBus.emit({\\n    type: \\\"run_started\\\",\\n    payload: { taskId, runId, prompt: tracked.prompt, status: \\\"running\\\", repoPath: repoPath ?? null, exclusions: exclusions ?? [] },\\n  });\\n\\n  console.log(`[tasks] coordinator executing for taskId=${taskId} (projectRoot=${repoPath ?? \\\"(default)\\\"})...`);\\n\\n  // The receipt promise is already running from submitWithGates\\n  gateResult.receipt.then((receipt) => {\\n    console.log(`[tasks] coordinator.submit resolved: taskId=${taskId}, verdict=${receipt.verdict}, cost=$${receipt.totalCost.estimatedCostUsd}`);\\n    tracked.runId = receipt.runId;\\n    tracked.status = receipt.verdict === \\\"success\\\" ? \\\"complete\\\" : receipt.verdict === \\\"aborted\\\" ? \\\"cancelled\\\" : \\\"failed\\\";\\n    tracked.completedAt = new Date().toISOString();\\n    tracked.receipt = receipt;\\n    void ctx.receiptStore.updateTask(taskId, {\\n      runId: receipt.runId,\\n      status: tracked.status,\\n      completedAt: tracked.completedAt,\\n      error: null,\\n    }).catch((err: unknown) =>\\n      console.error(\\\"[tasks] receiptStore update failed:\\\", err),\\n    );\\n\\n    ctx.eventBus.emit({\\n      type: \\\"run_complete\\\",\\n      payload: {\\n        taskId,\\n        runId: receipt.runId,\\n        verdict: receipt.verdict,\\n        totalCostUsd: receipt.totalCost.estimatedCostUsd,\\n        durationMs: receipt.durationMs,\\n        executionVerified: receipt.executionVerified,\\n        executionReason: receipt.executionGateReason,\\n      },\\n    });\\n\\n    ctx.eventBus.emit({\\n      type: \\\"run_receipt\\\",\\n      payload: { taskId, runId: receipt.runId, receiptId: receipt.id, receipt },\\n    });\\n  }).catch((err) => {\\n    console.error(\\\"═══ COORDINATOR SUBMIT FAILED ═══\\\");\\n    console.error(\\\"taskId:\\\", taskId);\\n    console.error(\\\"prompt:\\\", tracked.prompt.slice(0, 200));\\n    console.error(\\\"repoPath:\\\", repoPath ?? \\\"(default)\\\");\\n    console.error(\\\"error:\\\", err instanceof Error ? err.message : err);\\n    console.error(\\\"stack:\\\", err instanceof Error ? err.stack : \\\"\\\");\\n    console.error(\\\"═════════════════════════════════\\\");\\n\\n    tracked.status = \\\"failed\\\";\\n    tracked.completedAt = new Date().toISOString();\\n    tracked.error = err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err);\\n    void ctx.receiptStore.updateTask(taskId, {\\n      status: \\\"failed\\\",\\n      completedAt: tracked.completedAt,\\n      error: tracked.error,\\n    }).catch((err: unknown) =>\\n      console.error(\\\"[tasks] receiptStore update failed:\\\", err),\\n    );\\n\\n    ctx.eventBus.emit({\\n      type: \\\"run_complete\\\",\\n      payload: {\\n        taskId,\\n        runId,\\n        verdict: \\\"failed\\\",\\n        error: tracked.error,\\n        executionVerified: false,\\n      },\\n    });\\n  });\\n\\n  return { kind: \\\"running\\\", taskId, runId, prompt, repoPath: repoPath ?? null };\\n}\\n\\n/**\\n * Find the most recent tracked run so the unified Loqui route can\\n * double-checks so a\\n * stale client context cannot spawn a resume against nothing.\\n */\\nfunction findLatestTrackedRun(): TrackedRun | null {\\n  let latest: TrackedRun | null = null;\\n  for (const run of trackedRuns.values()) {\\n    if (!latest) {\\n      latest = run;\\n      continue;\\n    }\\n    if (run.submittedAt > latest.submittedAt) {\\n      latest = run;\\n    }\\n  }\\n  return latest;\\n}\\n\\nasync function findLatestKnownRun(ctx: ServerContext): Promise<{\\n  taskId: string | null;\\n  runId: string;\\n  prompt: string;\\n  status: string;\\n} | null> {\\n  const tracked = findLatestTrackedRun();\\n  if (tracked) {\\n    return {\\n      taskId: tracked.taskId,\\n      runId: tracked.runId,\\n      prompt: tracked.prompt,\\n      status: tracked.status,\\n    };\\n  }\\n\\n  const recent = await ctx.receiptStore.listRuns(10);\\n  for (const entry of recent) {\\n    const receipt = await ctx.receiptStore.getRun(entry.runId);\\n    if (!receipt) continue;\\n    const task = await ctx.receiptStore.getTaskByRunId(entry.runId);\\n    return {\\n      taskId: task?.taskId ?? null,\\n      runId: entry.runId,\\n      prompt: task?.prompt ?? receipt.prompt,\\n      status: receipt.status,\\n    };\\n  }\\n\\n  return null;\\n}\\n\\nasync function findKnownRunById(\\n  ctx: ServerContext,\\n  id: string | null | undefined,\\n): Promise<{\\n  taskId: string | null;\\n  runId: string;\\n  prompt: string;\\n  status: string;\\n} | null> {\\n  if (!id) return null;\\n  const task = await ctx.receiptStore.getTask(id);\\n  if (task) {\\n    const receipt = await ctx.receiptStore.getRun(task.runId);\\n    return {\\n      taskId: task.taskId,\\n      runId: task.runId,\\n      prompt: task.prompt || receipt?.prompt || \\\"\\\",\\n      status: receipt?.status ?? task.status,\\n    };\\n  }\\n  const byRun = await ctx.receiptStore.getTaskByRunId(id);\\n  if (byRun) {\\n    const receipt = await ctx.receiptStore.getRun(byRun.runId);\\n    return {\\n      taskId: byRun.taskId,\\n      runId: byRun.runId,\\n      prompt: byRun.prompt || receipt?.prompt || \\\"\\\",\\n      status: receipt?.status ?? byRun.status,\\n    };\\n  }\\n  const receipt = await ctx.receiptStore.getRun(id);\\n  if (receipt) {\\n    return {\\n      taskId: null,\\n      runId: receipt.runId,\\n      prompt: receipt.prompt,\\n      status: receipt.status,\\n    };\\n  }\\n  return null;\\n}\\n\\n// ─── Routes ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n\\nexport const taskRoutes: FastifyPluginAsync = async (fastify) => {\\n  const ctx = (): ServerContext => fastify.ctx;\\n\\n  async function cancelTrackedTask(id: string): Promise<{\\n    ok: boolean;\\n    taskId: string;\\n    runId: string | null;\\n    message: string;\\n  }> {\\n    const tracked = trackedRuns.get(id);\\n    if (tracked && tracked.runId) {\\n      const cancelled = ctx().coordinator.cancel(tracked.runId);\\n      if (cancelled) {\\n        tracked.status = \\\"cancelled\\\";\\n        tracked.completedAt = new Date().toISOString();\\n        tracked.error = \\\"Cancelled by user\\\";\\n        await ctx().receiptStore.updateTask(tracked.taskId, {\\n          status: \\\"cancelled\\\",\\n          completedAt: tracked.completedAt,\\n          error: tracked.error,\\n        });\\n        ctx().eventBus.emit({\\n          type: \\\"run_cancelled\\\",\\n          payload: {\\n            taskId: tracked.taskId,\\n            runId: tracked.runId,\\n            status:     ok: true,\\n          taskId: tracked.taskId,\\n          runId: tracked.runId,\\n          message: \\\"Run cancellation requested\\\",\\n        };\\n      }\\n    }\\n\\n    const persistedTask = await ctx().receiptStore.getTask(id) ?? await ctx().receiptStore.getTaskByRunId(id);\\n    if (persistedTask) {\\n      const cancelled = ctx().coordinator.cancel(persistedTask.runId);\\n      if (cancelled) {\\n        const completedAt = new Date().toISOString();\\n        await ctx().receiptStore.updateTask(persistedTask.taskId, {\\n          status: \\\"cancelled\\\",\\n          completedAt,\\n          error: \\\"Cancelled by user\\\",\\n        });\\n        const trackedPersisted = trackedRuns.get(persistedTask.taskId);\\n        if (trackedPersisted) {\\n          trackedPersisted.status = \\\"cancelled\\\";\\n          trackedPersisted.completedAt = completedAt;\\n          trackedPersisted.error = \\\"Cancelled by user\\\";\\n        }\\n        ctx().eventBus.emit({\\n          type: \\\"run_cancelled\\\",\\n          payload: {\\n            taskId: persistedTask.taskId,\\n            runId: persistedTask.runId,\\n            status: \\\"cancelled\\\",\\n            verdict: \\\"cancelled\\\",\\n            completedAt,\\n            error: \\\"Cancelled by user\\\",\\n          },\\n        });\\n        return {\\n          ok: true,\\n          taskId: persistedTask.taskId,\\n          runId: persistedTask.runId,\\n          message: \\\"Run cancellation requested\\\",\\n        };\\n      }\\n    }\\n\\n    const cancelled = ctx().coordinator.cancel(id);\\n    if (cancelled) {\\n      const completedAt = new Date().toISOString();\\n      ctx().eventBus.emit({\\n        type: \\\"run_cancelled\\\",\\n        payload: {\\n          taskId: id,\\n          runId: id,\\n          status: \\\"cancelled\\\",\\n          verdict: \\\"cancelled\\\",\\n          completedAt,\\n          error: \\\"Cancelled by user\\\",\\n        },\\n      });\\n      return {\\n        ok: true,\\n        taskId: id,\\n        runId: id,\\n        message: \\\"Run cancellation requested\\\",\\n      };\\n    }\\n\\n    return {\\n      ok: false,\\n      taskId: id,\\n      runId: null,\\n      message: `No active run found with ID \\\"${id}\\\"`,\\n    };\\n  }\\n\\n  function toTaskStatus(\\n    status: \\\"RUNNING\\\" | \\\"COMPLETE\\\" | \\\"FAILED\\\" | \\\"ABORTED\\\" | \\\"CRASHED\\\" | \\\"INTERRUPTED\\\",\\n  ): TrackedRun[\\\"status\\\"] {\\n    switch (status) {\\n      case \\\"RUNNING\\\":\\n        return \\\"running\\\";\\n      case \\\"COMPLETE\\\":\\n        return \\\"complete\\\";\\n      case \\\"ABORTED\\\":\\n      case \\\"INTERRUPTED\\\":\\n        return \\\"cancelled\\\";\\n      case \\\"CRASHED\\\":\\n      case \\\"FAILED\\\":\\n      default:\\n        return \\\"failed\\\";\\n    }\\n  }\\n\\n  fastify.post<{ Body: LoquiBody }>(\\n    \\\"/loqui\\\",\\n    {\\n      schema: {\\n        body: {\\n          type: \\\"object\\\",\\n          required: [\\\"question\\\", \\\"repoPath\\\"],\\n          properties: {\\n            question: { type: \\\"string\\\", minLength: 1 },\\n            repoPath: { type: \\\"string\\\", minLength: 1 },\\n          },\\n        },\\n      },\\n    },\\n    async (request: FastifyRequest<{ Body: LoquiBody }>, reply: FastifyReply) => {\\n      const { question, repoPath } = request.body;\\n\\n      if (!existsSync(repoPath)) {\\n        reply.code(400).send({\\n          error: \\\"Bad request\\\",\\n          message: `repoPath does not exist on this host: ${repoPath}`,\\n        });\\n        return;\\n      }\\n\\n      const answer = await askLoqui(question, repoPath);\\n      reply.send({ answer });\\n    }\\n  );\\n\\n  /**\\n   * POST /tasks/loqui/unified — Unified Loqui intent routing.\\n   *\\n   * Accepts one freeform input string, runs it through the\\n   * classifier + router, and dispatches to the correct backend path.\\n   * The UI sends every Loqui chat message here — there is no more\\n   * \\\"mode\\\" to pick at submit time.\\n   *\\n   * Response shape (always the same top-level wrapper):\\n   *   {\\n   *     route: \\\"build\\\" | \\\"answer\\\" | \\\"clarify\\\",\\n   *     intent, label, reason, confidence, signals,\\n   *     ...plus a route-specific payload:\\n   *       build   → { task_id, run_id, prompt, repo_path }\\n   *       answer  → { answer }\\n   *       clarify → { clarification }\\n   *   }\\n   *\\n   * The `signals` field exists so the UI (and later audit tooling)\\n   * can show *why* Loqui picked the route it picked — this preserves\\n   * the inspectability constraint from the execution-truth work.\\n   */\\n  fastify.post<{ Body: LoquiUnifiedBody }>(\\n    \\\"/loqui/unified\\\",\\n    {\\n      schema: {\\n        body: {\\n          type: \\\"object\\\",\\n          required: [\\\"input\\\", \\\"repoPath\\\"],\\n          properties: {\\n            input: { type: \\\"string\\\", minLength: 1 },\\n            repoPath: { type: \\\"string\\\", minLength: 1 },\\n            context: {\\n              type: \\\"object\\\",\\n              additionalProperties: true,\\n              properties: {\\n                activeRunId: { type: [\\\"string\\\", \\\"null\\\"] },\\n                lastRunId: { type: [\\\"string\\\", \\\"null\\\"] },\\n                lastRunVerdict: { type: [\\\"string\\\", \\\"null\\\"] },\\n                previousMessageWasBuild: { type: \\\"boolean\\\" },\\n              },\\n            },\\n          },\\n        },\\n      },\\n    },\\n    async (request: FastifyRequest<{ Body: LoquiUnifiedBody }>, reply: FastifyReply) => {\\n      const { \\\"cancelled\\\";\\n      case \\\"CRASHED\\\":\\n      case \\\"FAILED\\\":\\n      default:\\n        return \\\"failed\\\";\\n    }\\n  }\\n\\n  fastify.post<{ Body: LoquiBody }>(\\n    \\\"/loqui\\\",\\n    {\\n      schema: {\\n        body: {\\n          type: \\\"object\\\",\\n          required: [\\\"question\\\", \\\"repoPath\\\"],\\n          properties: {\\n            question: { type: \\\"string\\\", minLength: 1 },\\n            repoPath: { type: \\\"string\\\", minLength: 1 },\\n          },\\n        },\\n      },\\n    },\\n    async (request: FastifyRequest<{ Body: LoquiBody }>, reply: FastifyReply) => {\\n      const { question, repoPath } = request.body;\\n\\n      if (!existsSync(repoPath)) {\\n        reply.code(400).send({\\n          error: \\\"Bad request\\\",\\n          message: `repoPath does not exist on this host: ${repoPath}`,\\n        });\\n        return;\\n      }\\n\\n      const answer = await askLoqui(question, repoPath);\\n      reply.send({ answer });\\n    }\\n  );\\n\\n  /**\\n   * POST /tasks/loqui/unified — Unified Loqui intent routing.\\n   *\\n   * Accepts one freeform input string, runs it through the\\n   * fastify.post<{ Body: LoquiUnifiedBody }>(\\n    \\\"/loqui/unified\\\",\\n    {\\n      schema: {\\n        body: {\\n          type: \\\"object\\\",\\n          required: [\\\"input\\\", \\\"repoPath\\\"],\\n          properties: {\\n            input: { type: \\\"string\\\", minLength: 1 },\\n            repoPath: { type: \\\"string\\\", minLength: 1 },\\n            context: {\\n              type: \\\"object\\\",\\n              additionalProperties: true,\\n              properties: {\\n                activeRunId: { type: [\\\"string\\\", \\\"null\\\"] },\\n                lastRunId: { type: [\\\"string\\\", \\\"null\\\"] },\\n                lastRunVerdict: { type: [\\\"string\\\", \\\"null\\\"] },\\n                previousMessageWasBuild: { type: \\\"boolean\\\" },\\n              },\\n            },\\n          },\\n        },\\n      },\\n    },\\n    async (request: FastifyRequest<{ Body: LoquiUnifiedBody }>, reply: FastifyReply) => {\\n      const { input, repoPath } = request.body;\\n      const routerContext: LoquiIntentContext = request.body.context ?? {};\\n\\n      if (!existsSync(repoPath)) {\\n        reply.code(400).send({\\n          error: \\\"Bad request\\\",\\n          message: `repoPath does not exist on this host: ${repoPath}`,\\n        });\\n        return;\\n      }\\n\\n      const decision: LoquiRouteDecision = routeLoquiInput({ input, context: routerContext });\\n      console.log(\\n        `[tasks] /loqui/unified: action=${decision.action} intent=${decision.intent} ` +\\n        `label=${decision.label} confidence=${decision.confidence.toFixed(2)} ` +\\n        `reason=\\\"${decision.reason}\\\" signals=${decision.signals.length}`,\\n      );\\n\\n      // Base envelope every response shares. Gives the UI a stable\\n      // shape to hang its intent badge / signal audit off of, no\\n      // matter which backend path actually ran.\\n      const envelope = {\\n        route: decision.action,\\n        intent: decision.intent,\\n        label: decision.label,\\n        reason: decision.reason,\\n        confidence: decision.confidence,\\n        signals: [...decision.signals],\\n        original_input: decision.originalInput,\\n      };\\n\\n      switch (decision.action) {\\n        case \\\"build\\\": {\\n          const result = await submitBuildTask(ctx(), decision.effectivePrompt, repoPath, undefined);\\n          if (result.kind === \\\"needs_clarification\\\") {\\n            reply.send({\\n              ...envelope,\\n              route: \\\"clarify\\\",\\n              status: \\\"needs_clarification\\\",\\n              clarification: result.question,\\n            });\\n            return;\\n          }\\n          if (result.kind === \\\"needs_decomposition\\\") {\\n            reply.code(202).send({\\n              ...envelope,\\n              status: \\\"needs_decomposition\\\",\\n              task_id: result.taskId,\\n              plan: result.plan,\\n              message: result.message,\\n            });\\n            return;\\n          }\\n          reply.code(202).send({\\n            ...envelope,\\n            task_id: result.taskId,\\n            run_id: result.runId,\\n            prompt: result.prompt,\\n            repo_path: result.repoPath,\\n            status: \\\"running\\\",\\n            message: \\\"Build task submitted. Watch the worker grid and Lumen log for progress.\\\",\\n          });\\n          return;\\n        }\\n\\n        case \\\"resume\\\": {\\n          // Stitch the new input onto the most recent tracked run's\\n          // prompt so the Coordinator sees a self-contained request.\\n          // If no prior run exists on the server (e.g. because this\\n          // server was restarted since the UI learned about it), we\\n          // fall back to treating the input as a bare build rather\\n          // than failing — the user's intent to \\\"continue\\\" is clear\\n          // even if we don't have the history to reference.\\n          const requestedPriorId = routerContext.activeRunId ?? routerContext.lastRunId ?? null;\\n          const prior =\\n            await findKnownRunById(ctx(), requestedPriorId) ??\\n            await findLatestKnownRun(ctx());\\n          const stitched = prior\\n            ? `Continue the prior build (${prior.runId}): \\\"${prior.prompt}\\\". New build task submitted.`\\n              : \\\"No prior run found in persisted history — submitted as a fresh build.\\\",\\n          });\\n          return;\\n        }\\n\\n        case \\\"answer\\\": {\\n          const answer = await askLoqui(decision.effectivePrompt, repoPath);\\n          reply.send({ ...envelope, answer });\\n          return;\\n        }\\n\\n        case \\\"dry_run\\\": {\\n          // Preflight + Dry Run System v1 — grounded structured\\n          // plan produced by generateDryRun. The plan reuses every planning\\n          // primitive the Coordinator uses at runtime, so the\\n          // steps the user sees here match what would happen if\\n          // they submitted the same request for real.\\n          const plan = generateDryRun({ input: decision.originalInput, projectRoot: repoPath });\\n          reply.send({ ...envelope, plan });\\n          return;\\n        }\\n\\n        case \\\"clarify\\\":\\n        default: {\\n          reply.send({ ...envelope, clarification: decision.clarification });\\n          return;\\n        }\\n      }\\n    }\\n  );\\n\\n  /**\\n   * POST /tasks/dry-run — Direct dry-run entry point.\\n   *\\n   * Skips the classifier and produces a structured dry-run plan\\n   * from a raw prompt + repoPath. Used by API clients that\\n   * already know they want a plan (no natural-language gate).\\n   * Response shape mirrors the `dry_run` action from the\\n   * unified route: { plan: DryRunPlan }.\\n   */\\n  fastify.post<{ Body: { input: string; repoPath: string } }>(\\n    \\\"/dry-run\\\",\\n    {\\n      schema: {\\n        body: {\\n          type: \\\"object\\\",\\n          required: [\\\"input\\\", \\\"repoPath\\\"],\\n          properties: {\\n            input: { type: \\\"string\\\", minLength: 1 },\\n            repoPath: { type: \\\"string\\\", minLength: 1 },\\n          },\\n        },\\n      },\\n    },\\n    async (request, reply) => {\\n      const { input, repoPath } = request.body;\\n      if (!existsSync(repoPath)) {\\n        reply.code(400).send({\\n          error: \\\"Bad request\\\",\\n          message: `repoPath does not exist on this host: ${repoPath}`,\\n        });\\n        return;\\n      }\\n      const plan = generateDryRun({ input, projectRoot: repoPath });\\n      reply.send({ plan });\\n    }\\n  );\\n\\n  /**\\n   * POST /tasks — Submit a new build task.\\n   *\\n   * Calls coordinator.submit() and tracks the run.\\n   * Returns immediately with task_id + run_id. The run executes async.\\n   * Client should subscribe on WebSocket with { type: \\\"subscribe\\\", runId }.\\n   *\\n   * The optional `repoPath` field in the body lets the caller target a\\n   * specific local repo as the project root for this build.
